data.title: Dye & Durham names Sid Singh as interim CEO
data.description:
• Dye & Durham (TSX:DND:CA) said it has named Sid Singh as its interim CEO, effective immediately.
  • Singh will remain a director and will succeed Hans Gieskes in the role.
  • The company also appointed Arnaud Ajdler as the chair.
  • Singh has over 25 years of leadership experience and was, most recently, the CEO of Rectangle Health.

data.title: Dorel Industries reports financing transaction
data.description:
• Dorel Industries (OTCPK:DIIBF) Friday said that it has entered into a sale-leaseback transaction for its factory and warehousing facility in Columbus, Indiana.
  • The gross proceeds to Dorel from the sale will be $30 million, of which approximately $8 million will be allocated to reduce existing debt, with the balance designated for funding the company’s ongoing operations.
  • The lease has an initial term of ten years and may be renewed at the company’s option for two renewal terms of five years each.
  • The initial annual rent is approximately $2.9 million, subject to annual increases.
  • Dorel Industries (OTCPK:DIIBF) said it is exploring additional opportunities to further enhance its financial position.

data.title: Teva/Alvotech follow Amgen in launching biosimilar for J&J’s Stelara
data.description:
Teva Pharmaceutical (NYSE:TEVA) and Alvotech (NASDAQ:ALVO) have launched their biosimilar targeting Johnson & Johnson’s (NYSE:JNJ) blockbuster arthritis therapy Stelara (ustekinumab) in the U.S. weeks after Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN) became the first to launch a Stelara copycat version.

Teva (NYSE:TEVA) and Alvotech’s (NASDAQ:ALVO) biosimilar branded as Selarsdi will be available across four approved presentations for inflammatory conditions such as psoriatic arthritis, plaque psoriasis, Crohn’s disease, and ulcerative colitis, the companies announced Friday.

Once the exclusivity for the first interchangeable biosimilar of Stelara expires on Apr. 30, Selarsdi will be marketed with interchangeability designation, meaning pharmacists will be able to switch patients from the reference product to the biosimilar without the intervention of a prescriber.

Selarsdi represents the second biosimilar to reach the U.S. market as part of a partnership between the companies inked in 2020 to commercialize Alvotech’s (NASDAQ:ALVO) biosimilars. Teva (NYSE:TEVA) and Alvotech (ALVO) launched a biosimilar targeting AbbVie’s (ABBV) arthritis therapy Humira in May 2024 as the first product under the collaboration.

In January, Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN) partnered with UnitedHealth (UNH) unit Optum Health to launch the first U.S. copycat version against Stelara, sales of which reached $10.4B in 2024 with a ~5% YoY drop amid biosimilar launches in Europe.

data.title: Balchem Non-GAAP EPS of $1.13 beats by $0.03, revenue of $240M in-line
data.description:
• Balchem press release (NASDAQ:BCPC): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.13 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $240M (+4.9% Y/Y) in-line.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $62.8 million.
  • Cash flows from operations were $52.3 million, with free cash flow of $39.8 million.

data.title: Relevant Gold upsizes private placement to C$7M
data.description:
• Relevant Gold (OTCQB:RGCCF) said it has upped the size of its private placement to 23.3M shares at C$0.30 apiece.
  • The firm is now expected to raise C$7M, and the proceeds will be used to fund exploration activities at its projects in Wyoming, USA, and for general working capital.
  • The company added that certain directors and officers of the firm may acquire securities under the private placement.

data.title: Faraday Future reports shipment of first two FX 6 camouflaged prototype mules to U.S., shares up
data.description:
Faraday Future Intelligent Electric (NASDAQ:FFIE) Friday said that its subsidiary, FF China, has shipped two FX 6 camouflaged prototype mules to customs, marking the next step in their journey to the company’s U.S. headquarters in Los Angeles.

FFIE is up +2.11% to $1.70 in premarket trade.

The FX 6 is part of FF’s broader FX product strategy aimed at delivering Advanced Intelligent Electric Vehicles (AIEVs) to a mass-market audience, the company said.

The FX brand will target the mass market segment with three planned models: an AI-MPV product—named the Super One, the FX 5, with a price target between $20,000-$30,000, and the FX 6, with a price target between $30,000-$50,000.

FF will announce further updates on the FX 6 series and its overall FX strategy in March.

data.title: Telix Pharmaceuticals reports FY results; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Telix Pharmaceuticals press release (NASDAQ:TLX): FY Adjusted EBITDA of A$99.3 million, up by 70%, demonstrating strong underlying growth
  • Revenue of A$783.2M (+55.9% Y/Y).

  • Guidance: Telix provides FY2025 revenue guidance of A$1.18 billion to A$1.23 billion (US$770 million to US$800 million) vs. consensus estimates of $788.65 million. This guidance includes revenue from Illuccix (in jurisdictions with a marketing authorization) and 11 months of revenue from RLS (and excluding RLS revenue generated from Illuccix). Guidance does not reflect revenue for products that have not yet received a marketing authorization (for example, Gozellix, Pixclara and Zircaix).

  • Telix has also provided R&D expenditure guidance, expecting an increased investment range of 20% to 25% compared to FY2024.

data.title: Europe markets slightly higher as investors read flash PMI numbers
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects market movements as of 6.45am ET.

London (UKX) +0.12% to 8,673. UK's flash manufacturing PMI hits 14-month low of 46.4 in February.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.20% to 22,358, Germany's flash PMI shows growth in service sector, while manufacturing struggles. Additionally, attention turned to upcoming elections on Sunday, with the center-right CDU leading the polls, while Chancellor Scholz's center-left SPD has fallen to third place.

France (CAC:IND) +0.55% to 8,167, France flash PMI readings shows worst decline in economic output since Sept 2023. France’s business climate indicator rose to 96 in February.

The consumer confidence indicator in the Czech Republic fell to 96.6 in February. The business confidence indicator in the Czech Republic increased to 98.

Euro Area services PMI at 3-month low in Feb, manufacturers cut purchasing activity amid weak demand.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) edged 0.59% higher to 554.20, after a lackluster session the previous day.

In corporates, Standard Chartered (OTCPK:SCBFF) shares surged after the British bank reported a decline in Q4 net profit but announced a $1.5 billion share buyback. Leonardo (DRS) also posted results that exceeded expectations.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 1 basis point to 4.49%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 5 basis points to 2.49%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 1 basis point at 4.60%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK).

data.title: Europe trumps the U.S. in terms of stock market returns. Will it last?
data.description:
There has been a lot of pessimism about Europe, given stalling growth, rising costs, tariff threats and the war in Ukraine. Former economic engine Germany has stagnated for two consecutive years and things don't look much better in France, Italy, the U.K., or elsewhere. Interestingly, European stock markets have performed well as of late, even beating the U.S. since the beginning of the year.

Snapshot: Increased military spending pushed the region's defense stocks to all-time highs on Monday, while the STOXX Europe 600 Index (STOXX) has already climbed 8.6% in 2025, more than double the 4% return of the S&P 500 (SP500). Things look even better for the Euro Stoxx 50 Index (SX5E), which is up more than 14% YTD, and even for France and Germany, where the CAC 40 (CAC:IND) and DAX (DAX:IND) are ahead by 10.4% and 12.4% YTD. This bullishness has surprised many, especially when many nations in Europe are mired in political volatility.

What's going on? European central banks are expected to continue easing as the Federal Reserve stays on pause, while Ukraine ceasefire talks are underway, as well as a push toward stimulus in prior fiscally disciplined countries. European markets are also way smaller than the U.S., so even a little bit of diversification money being transferred there will show an outsized impact. At the recent World Economic Forum, BlackRock's (BLK) Larry Fink called investment in Europe a counter-trade, saying, "Always go against Davos and you'll make a lot of money."

Elsewhere: Germans are going to the polls this weekend, with the CDU's Friedrich Merz poised to be the next chancellor, though he may face a difficult time forming a governing majority due to the rise of Alternative for Germany (AfD). On the ballot are stances over migration, as well as economic malaise prompted by the loss of industry and cheap Russian energy. For investors, there also might be more of a spotlight on reforms to the Schuldenbremse, or debt brake, to finance structural investments for the economy. In terms of foreign markets, Seeking Alpha subscribers actually see more opportunity in India compared to other regions for investment, according to the latest SA Sentiment Survey, though Europe came in second place. Will the gains last?

data.title: First National Bank Alaska GAAP EPS of $6.29, total interest and loan fee income of $63.4M
data.description:
• First National Bank Alaska press release (OTCQX:FBAK): Q4 GAAP EPS of $6.29.
  • Total interest and loan fee income of $63.4M (+6.2% Y/Y).
  • Provision for income taxes was reduced $2.2 million in the fourth quarter of 2024 as compared to the fourth quarter of 2023, reflecting certain state income tax benefits achieved in the securities portfolio.
  • Return on equity as of Dec. 31, 2024, was 13.60% compared to 13.97% as of Dec. 31, 2023.
  • Book value per share as increased to $163.11, compared to $146.77 as of Dec. 31, 2023.
  • The bank’s Dec. 31, 2024, Tier 1 leverage capital ratio of 10.54% remains above well-capitalized standards.
